page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.12xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8369HB processors running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. With a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, each of the 48 vCPUs corresponds to a dedicated physical core, ensuring consistent compute performance without resource sharing or hyperthreading overhead.

The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-CPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This configuration lacks local NVMe storage and does not include integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps, facilitating efficient data transfer for distributed workloads.

This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive enterprise applications that require dedicated CPU resources and high memory density without the need for local SSD storage. The dedicated core allocation makes it suitable for workloads sensitive to noisy-neighbor effects. Typical use cases include in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-heavy data processing pipelines.

With a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, it provides dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading. The instance is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-CPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. This resource profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring dedicated CPU performance.
